Linux/macOS/WSL:

    If you already have a key: cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub (RSA) or cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub (Ed25519)
    If not found: run
        ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 -C ken@Akuma
        Then: cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub
Next, copy the public key content and paste it into ken's authorized_keys (on the OpenClaw VM) as shown:

    On the VM:
        mkdir -p /home/ken/.ssh
        chmod 700 /home/ken/.ssh
        echo "<your-public-key>" >> /home/ken/.ssh/authorized_keys
        chmod 600 /home/ken/.ssh/authorized_keys
        chown -R ken:ken /home/ken/.ssh

        ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 -C ken@Akuma

ssh-rsa  ken@Akuma

To add that public key to ken's authorized_keys on 172.16.13.1:

If you can log in as ken now:
- mkdir -p /home/ken/.ssh
- chmod 700 /home/ken/.ssh
- cat >> /home/ken/.ssh/authorized_keys << 'KEY'
<paste your full public key here>
KEY
- chmod 600 /home/ken/.ssh/authorized_keys
- chown -R ken:ken /home/ken

If you can't log in but have another admin path:
- On a reachable host, run on the remote (as root or with sudo):
  - mkdir -p /home/ken/.ssh
  - echo '<your-public-key>' | sudo tee -a /home/ken/.ssh/authorized_keys
  - sudo chmod 700 /home/ken/.ssh
  - sudo chmod 600 /home/ken/.ssh/authorized_keys
  - sudo chown -R ken:ken /home/ken
Here's how to add the public key you provided to ken's account on the OpenClaw VM (172.16.13.1).

If you can log in as an admin on the host or as ken:
- Create the .ssh directory and add the key:
  - mkdir -p /home/ken/.ssh
  - chmod 700 /home/ken/.ssh
  - cat >> /home/ken/.ssh/authorized_keys << 'SSHKEY'
  ssh-rsa  ken@Akuma
  SSHKEY
  - chmod 600 /home/ken/.ssh/authorized_keys
  - chown -R ken:ken /home/ken/.ssh

If ken doesn't exist yet (you have admin access elsewhere and need to create the user):
- On the remote host:
  - sudo useradd -m -d /home/ken -s /bin/bash ken
  - sudo mkdir -p /home/ken/.ssh
  - sudo bash -lc 'cat >> /home/ken/.ssh/authorized_keys' << 'SSHKEY'
  ssh-rsa  ken@Akuma
  SSHKEY
  - sudo chmod 700 /home/ken/.ssh
  - sudo chmod 600 /home/ken/.ssh/authorized_keys
  - sudo chown -R ken:ken /home/ken/.ssh

If you share your public key here, I'll craft the exact commands for you. Also ensure SSHD on 172.16.13.1 allows PubkeyAuthentication (and/or PasswordAuthentication) and that ken is allowed to log in.
